session_note: S74 (user-approved Micro PDR). P1 = ADD e2e/bomb.spec.ts to the gating lane — the S71 pickup-bomb is unit-only; hunter + potato each have an e2e. Test-debt closure; the __TEST_BOMB_SPAWN_SPARKS__ seam already exists. NO production code changes; PROTOCOL_VERSION stays 5.
council_battle_ledger: S73 P1 Standard 3-way Council (Claude+Grok-4.20-reasoning+Gemini-2.5-pro); both ADOPT-WITH-CHANGES. ADOPTED: stateful sequence-fold test (reconcile is PURE -> fold over join/join/leave-middle/back-fill/Begin; the real regression surface) + seat-uniqueness/host-0/seats-in-[1,MAX-1] invariant assertion (Grok G4) + no-hole stable==dense canary. PARTIAL: Grok G1 NEW-peer ordering -- REJECT the canonical peerId-sort (arrival-order = peerSet insertion order is the intuitive contract; lobby seating is NOT replayed by save.replay), adopt explicit doc-it. REJECT: Gemini Ge4 PROTOCOL_VERSION bump -- premise factual error (the roster is a COMPACT array of OCCUPIED seats with a non-contiguous seat FIELD, NOT a sparse/null array; isValidRoster protocol.ts:329 has no contiguity check; the client renders the RECEIVED roster via lobbyView bySeat.get(i) lobbyStateMachine.ts:329, never a local positional recompute; Begin START_GAME_SIGNAL stays dense) -> no-bump per the S70 cosmetic-presence precedent + friends-only single-deploy. CARRY-FORWARD #4: TOCTOU late-leave at Begin (PRE-EXISTING, not worsened -- clients seat from msg.roster not local peerIds), host-migration must init lobbySeats fresh, Option-2 sparse-in-game (spawn-by-ordinal). DECISIONS: Q1 1b single-source-of-truth (1a diverges on back-fill -- both reviewers concede); Q2 Option 1 dense Begin (Option 2 deferred); Q3 lowest-free fill; Q4 pure fold (no Pixi extraction).
prime_audit: S74 PRIME-AUDIT (Micro): the GROK-ANALYST FIX-THEN-SHIP pass served as the adversarial self-audit. 5 findings explicitly triaged - adopt 1/2/4 (build-vs-bomb race + tight bomb-wait fixed via the dtSec-clamp root-cause confirmed in main.ts:496 + test.setTimeout/90s-wait/build-margin; grab retry), 3 covered by the retry, 5 REFUTED (zone-membership-contract reasoning vs brittle 0.85-inset coupling). Runtime-verifiability: the spec IS a boot-then-smoke (Playwright dev-server + live __SPARK__ reads), not a static parse. No consensus-masking (single analyst). Mtime N/A. Confidence HIGH.
council_waiver_reason: Micro-tier test-debt closure (1 NEW e2e spec file, ZERO production code; the seam exists). Micro deliberation auto-waived on the user-approval path. CHECK = RALPH:PATROL + GROK-ANALYST (Micro), not full Triumvirate.

priorities.label: S74 P1 (Micro) -- ADD e2e/bomb.spec.ts (GATING lane, solo/host-authoritative, no @quarantine-flaky), modeled on potato.spec.ts/hunter.spec.ts. Proves the e2e-unique LIVE-BROWSER wiring that tsc+unit are blind to: (1) spawner.tick -> physicsLoop SPAWN_BOMB dispatch puts a bomb into world.bombs on its cadence (__TEST_BOMB_SPAWN_SPARKS__ small + fast spawn + __TEST_WIN_SCORE__ 999); (2) controls.onDown -> pickBomb -> TRIGGER_BOMB -> applyTriggerBomb: a real LMB click on the orb removes the bomb AND severs bonds on a pre-built same-color cluster (bonds.length drops). 0 page errors. Local readBombs/waitForBomb helpers (mirror readPotatoes/waitForPotato); reuse helpers.ts. NO production code. PROTOCOL_VERSION stays 5. GATE: tsc 0 + Playwright gating lane 29 pass (27 existing + 2 new) + 1 pre-existing skip; vitest/knip/build unaffected (e2e excluded from bundle). CHECK Micro (RALPH:PATROL + GROK-ANALYST).
priorities.council_waiver_reason: Micro test-debt closure; user-approved; CHECK = RALPH + GROK-ANALYST.
priorities.check_method: S74 P1 (add e2e/bomb.spec.ts gating spec; Micro test-debt closure) VERIFIED + SHIPPED 7a0f4fb. GAP: the S71 pickup-bomb was the last hazard with NO dedicated Playwright e2e (hunter=hunter.spec.ts, potato=potato.spec.ts; bomb was unit-only via bombLifecycle.test.ts); the __TEST_BOMB_SPAWN_SPARKS__ seam (constants.ts:408-415) existed but was unused. ADDED 2 solo/host-authoritative GATING-lane tests (no @quarantine-flaky) modeled on potato/hunter, covering the e2e-unique LIVE-BROWSER wiring tsc+unit are blind to: (1) spawner.tick -> physicsLoop SPAWN_BOMB mints a bomb into world.bombs in the spawn zone on its cadence (asserts count===1 + positive radius + pos inside the 250px spawner disk); (2) controls.onDown -> pickBomb -> TRIGGER_BOMB -> applyTriggerBomb on a real LMB click removes the bomb (count->0) AND severs bonds on a pre-built >=2-bond same-color cluster (bonds.length drops). ZERO production code (seam pre-existed); PROTOCOL_VERSION stays 5. RACE-FREE BY CONSTRUCTION: the sever test builds the cluster BEFORE any bomb exists (cadence 24 sparks; the build provably consumes fewer in every sim-speed regime - fast sim ~10 sparks/5s build, slow sim spawns fewer during a longer build) + suppresses potatoes (__TEST_POTATO_SPAWN_SPARKS__=999) so NO spawner-zone mouse-down can grab a hazard mid-build (pickBomb/pickPotato take priority in onDown). GATES: tsc 0, knip 0, vitest 1110 (unchanged - e2e excluded from vitest glob), vite build 529.74KB (<550 charter, unchanged - e2e excluded from bundle), Playwright gating lane 29 pass (27 existing + 2 new) + 1 pre-existing Sym-E skip; new spec also validated in isolation 3x incl --repeat-each=2 (stable; sever test 14.5s). CHECK MICRO: RALPH:PATROL CLEAN (all gates green = backpressure sweep). GROK-ANALYST (grok-4.20-reasoning) FIX-THEN-SHIP, 5 findings TRIAGED against the actual loop code: findings 1+2 (build-vs-bomb race + tight bomb-wait under CI sim-clock slowdown) VALID - root-confirmed via main.ts:496 dtSec clamped to 50ms/frame (fixed-step accumulator => sim falls behind wall-clock under slow software-WebGL, so N sparks can take 2-4x nominal) => FIXED with test.setTimeout(120s) + 90s bomb-arrival wait + the build-<cadence margin; finding 4 (no grab retry) ADOPTED as a bounded 3-attempt grab loop; finding 3 (TTL TOCTOU) LOW (grabbed bomb is freshly-spawned w/ full 15s TTL, click <1s later) covered by the retry; finding 5 (hypot<=250 never hits the 0.85-inset disk edge ~212) REFUTED - <=SPAWNER_RADIUS IS the zone-membership contract; tightening to the 0.85 inset would be brittle against a non-bug tuning change. Re-validated post-fix (--repeat-each=2 all green) + final full gating lane 29 pass. PRIME-AUDIT: the GROK-ANALYST FIX-THEN-SHIP pass WAS the adversarial review (all findings explicitly adopt/refute-with-reason, no consensus-masking single-analyst); runtime-verifiability = the spec IS a real-browser boot-then-smoke (Playwright dev-server + live __SPARK__ world reads), not a static parse; mtime N/A (no leak/regression-landing claim). checkpoint 7a0f4fb (pushed; CI deploy+e2e running -> confirm green next boot per Rule 22).

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #bomb-e2e #test-debt-closure #gating-lane #sim-clock-slowdown #race-free-by-construction #grok-analyst-triage: shipped e2e/bomb.spec.ts (7a0f4fb) - the last hazard e2e (hunter+potato had one; bomb was unit-only). 2 solo gating tests: spawner SPAWN_BOMB cadence -> world.bombs, and a real LMB-click TRIGGER_BOMB -> bomb removed + bonds severed on a pre-built cluster. ZERO production code (the __TEST_BOMB_SPAWN_SPARKS__ seam pre-existed). DEFINING MOVES: (1) flakiness root-cause hunt - a spawner-zone mouse-down within a hazard's pick-radius fires pickBomb/pickPotato INSTEAD of picking the spark (onDown priority), so the sever test builds the bonded cluster BEFORE any bomb exists (cadence 24 > build-consumed sparks in every sim-speed regime) and suppresses potatoes outright, making the build race-free by CONSTRUCTION rather than by timing luck. (2) GROK-ANALYST CHECK (FIX-THEN-SHIP) flagged the bomb-wait as CI-flaky; I verified the cause by READING the loop (main.ts:496 clamps dtSec to 50ms/frame - a fixed-step accumulator, so under slow software-WebGL the sim clock falls behind wall-clock and 'N sparks' can take 2-4x nominal) rather than guessing, then fixed with test.setTimeout(120s)+90s arrival wait+bounded grab retry, and REFUTED the one brittle finding (tightening the in-zone assertion to the 0.85 spawn-inset) with the zone-membership-contract reasoning. LESSON: for an e2e gating test, design OUT the race (control WHEN the hazard exists relative to your clicks) instead of tuning timeouts to dodge it; and when a reviewer flags flakiness, confirm the mechanism in the actual loop code (the dtSec clamp) before choosing the fix. Validated --repeat-each=2 + full gating lane 29 pass + 1 pre-existing skip.

carry_forward: === RESOLVED S72 === P2 PAC-MAN HUNTER (97e4616): leader-reaches-75% spawns ONCE a host-authoritative hunter chasing the avatar via deterministic capped-momentum pursuit (juke-able); catch -> 30s bench (avatar hidden + input locked) + DROP_SPARK; 30s escape -> despawn. SEPARATE world.hunters Map; CRITICAL target-disconnect crash-guard; solo avatarPos fix; no protocol bump; additive snapshot. P3 POTATO BOMB (76af09a): carryable from-SPAWN-fuse (Fork E user) radial-AoE weapon; SEPARATE world.potatoes Map + separate potatoRng (spark+bomb byte-identical); carry-1 exclusive w/ spark (both directions); deterministic position-based AoE (squared-dist + sorted PrimitiveId/BondId; owner-agnostic; no chain reaction); carrier-disconnect force-detonate (sim-loop poll); NO protocol bump (Council batch-bump). P4 (309024c): detonation BOOM SFX (covers bomb+potato) + defensive applyStartGame bomb-clear. Teardown invariant for all 3 hazards on WIN/RETURN_TO_TITLE/START_GAME. === LOGGED CHECK CARRY-FORWARDS (S72, not dropped) === (a) NO-BUMP cosmetic mixed-version gap: a stale-v5 (P2-build) client joining a P3-build host sees prims vanish from a potato AoE without rendering the potato -- accepted friends-only trade-off (both CHECK reviewers validated no-bump); if a future feature needs cross-version safety, revisit the S52 new-client-intent->bump precedent. (b) Centralized PLAYING-exit teardown (Gemini P3): the current per-trigger (WIN_TRIGGER) + RETURN_TO_TITLE + START_GAME-invariant fully covers the actual FSM (no DRAW state), but a single PLAYING-exit teardown hook would be cleaner if the FSM grows. (c) spawner rng/bombRng/potatoRng not serialized -> save/load resets spawn cadence (PRE-EXISTING spark-spawner posture; folds into netcode/save-determinism backlog). (d) hunter disconnect-fade (PDR locks immediate despawn; optional polish). (e) Fork E potato fuse from-SPAWN is one-line-flippable to from-placement (makePotato + applyPlacePotato) if playtest prefers Council's. === CONFIRM AT NEXT BOOT (Rule 22) === P3 (76af09a) + P4 (309024c) post-push CI E2E were in-flight at handoff (local Playwright 27/27 green); confirm both green. === PRE-EXISTING BACKLOG (prioritized for next session; NOT started S72 -- ORANGE budget + each is multi-session) === 1. STABLE LOBBY SEATS (non-compacting) -- likely the most tractable + highest user-value next; bounded scope (lobby/seating). 2. CONTROL-MESSAGE SENDER-AUTH -- security hardening; bounded, raises the v1 threat model past friends-only. 3. EYES fog fuzzy-edge + CVD shape-icons -- render/accessibility feature; self-contained. 4. LIVE-PLAY NETCODE INFRA (host-migration / reconnect / 6p delta) -- LARGEST (genuinely multi-session); also the home for the spawner-rng-serialization + the no-bump cross-version work above. Recommend: tackle #1 or #3 as a single-priority Standard PDR next session; #4 needs its own multi-session plan. === OTHER (pre-existing) === main.ts Pixi/DOM shell hypertrophy (de-hypertrophy candidate).
